name: regime_shift_low_to_high
description: First 10 days: zero return (calm). Last 10 days: alternating +/- 0.02. Tests behavior when volatility regime changes mid-window. The 20-day lookback should weight every day equally (unweighted population stdev), so the mix dilutes the high-vol portion by half.
derivation: daily_returns = [0]*10 + [+2, -2, +2, -2, ...] (5 positive, 5 negative). Mean = 0. Variance = (0^2 * 10 + 2^2 * 10) / 20 = 40/20 = 2.0. std = sqrt(2.0) = 1.4142135623730951. Note: equal-weight stdev means the calm half does NOT vanish; it pulls the mean toward zero AND adds zero variance contribution, so the variance equals (10 * amplitude^2) / 20 = amplitude^2 / 2.
